title: Tapping in Depression: Small Acts
subtitle: A tapping practice for days when everything feels flat and starting anything feels out of reach
excerpt: A tapping practice for days when everything feels grey and small. Not for fixing the day, but for the next five minutes. One small act. The practice meets you where you are.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The EFT tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, developed in the mid-1990s.

practice.whenToUse: Use on days when everything feels flat and starting anything feels out of reach. This practice does not fix the day. It meets you in the moment and offers one small forward step.

content.text: A tapping practice for the days when everything feels flat. Not for fixing the whole day. Not for getting back to normal. For the next five minutes, and the one small act that might follow.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop (repeat x3)
Even though everything feels grey and small and I do not know how to start, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though the things that usually help are not reaching me right now, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I am tired in a way that sleep does not fix, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
**Eyebrow:** Everything feels flat.
**Side of eye:** The things that usually help are not reaching me right now.
**Under eye:** Getting out of bed was already something.
**Under nose:** I cannot do the whole day. I can maybe do the next five minutes.
**Chin:** I feel like I should be more than this.
**Collarbone:** But I am exactly where I am and that is what is true.
**Under arm:** One small act. That is all.
**Top of head:** I am still here and that is something.
Reframe to positive
**Eyebrow:** The small act counts.
**Side of eye:** Getting up counted. Being here counts.
**Under eye:** I do not have to fix the day. I only have to do the next thing.
**Under nose:** Small acts are real acts.
**Chin:** I am not behind. I am right here.
**Collarbone:** One thing at a time. One small thing.
**Under arm:** I am allowed to move slowly.
**Top of head:** I am doing what I can. That is enough for now.
Tap the round three times. Then tap the reframe three times.
Where this practice comes from
Original to homemade.education. EFT tapping was developed by Gary Craig in the 1990s.
